excerpt_text: Color-plus-rise features improve balanced-test AUC from 0.81 to 0.89 in the held-out alert split.
Table 2 reports the same AUC gain and confirms that the improvement is measured on a held-out balanced test set.
Replay triage raises follow-up queue purity from 0.46 to 0.71 for the top 20 simulated alerts.
The approach should transfer cleanly to Rubin-scale alert brokers with minimal retuning.
Training uses 38,412 alerts from a single ZTF-like broker snapshot and freezes all handcrafted quality gates.
For alerts with signal-to-noise below 7, the AUC gain shrinks to about 0.02 and the ranking becomes unstable.
The study does not test cross-survey domain shift, so transfer beyond the training broker remains unverified.
The appendix states a false-positive rate of 0.11 at fixed recall, but the exact threshold needs a page-level quote check.
The visual inspection claim is based on 30 manually reviewed alerts, so the sampling note should be checked before quoting it.
Most of the purity gain comes from removing obvious image artifacts before scoring, not from the ranker alone.
